title: Mammalian Cell Culture: Subculturing
author: Kenneth Schackart
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xukfnuw

steps.title: Under light microscope, look at the...
steps.instruction: Under light microscope, look at the cells and assess level of confluency. This is how you will determine the need to subculture. Confluency can be estimated by evaluating the percentage of surface covered by cells. Low confluency SH-SY5Y High confluency SH-SY5Y

steps.title: Using serological pipette, add 1 mL DPBS to T-25 flask
steps.instruction: Using serological pipette, add 1 mL DPBS to T-25 flask.

steps.title: Using serological pipette, remove DPBS and dispose into wase beaker
steps.instruction: Using serological pipette, remove DPBS and dispose into wase beaker.

steps.title: Repeat the above 2 steps, so...
steps.instruction: Repeat the above 2 steps, so that you will wash the cells twice. Always use a fresh pipette tip when drawing liquid from a stock solution.

steps.title: Add 1 mL warmed trypsin-EDTA to T-25 flask
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL warmed trypsin-EDTA to T-25 flask.

steps.title: Wait 00:05:00 for trypsin-EDTA to detach the cells
steps.instruction: Wait 00:05:00 for trypsin-EDTA to detach the cells. This time will vary in practice, and depends on cell type and trypsin concentration (i.e. 0.05% vs 0.25%). Some cell types may take up to 15 minutes. In those cases, assess detachment progress using a light microscope.

steps.title: Add 1 mL cell culture media
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL cell culture media. Trypsin-EDTA is neutralized by adding a volume of cell culture media equal to that of trypsin-EDTA.

steps.title: Using a serological pipette, transfer the...
steps.instruction: Using a serological pipette, transfer the cell suspension (cells, trypsin-EDTA, and cell culture media) into a 15 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the cell suspension on 1
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the cell suspension on 1.5 kRPM for 00:03:00 . This has been perfomed by T.A. ahead of time. Bring your cell suspension to trade for a spun down cell pellet. Centrifuge tubes with large cell pellets at the bottom and 8 mL of supernatant.

steps.title: Remove supernatant, dispose into waste beaker
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, dispose into waste beaker. You can leave a small amount with the serological pipette, the rest will be taken off in the next step.

steps.title: Using a 1000 μL pipette, carefully...
steps.instruction: Using a 1000 μL pipette, carefully remove the remaining supernatant, being cautious not to disturb the cell pellet. Always dispose of pipette tips in sharps container. Do not use the same tip twice.

steps.title: Add 1000 µL cell culture media...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL cell culture media to the cell pellet, and allow to sit for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Gently pipette mix the cell pellet...
steps.instruction: Gently pipette mix the cell pellet until the pellet is resuspended. Pipette mixing is done by slowly drawing in solution and pushing it out several times, all without removing the pipette tip from the solution. Ask T.A. for help on this if you need some pointers.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 mL fresh cell culture media to T-25 flask, this will preserve any remaining cells.

steps.title: Reseed your T-25 flask by transferring...
steps.instruction: Reseed your T-25 flask by transferring 500 µL cell suspension to the flask. In practice you would reseed the other 500 µL into another flask, or even split the cell suspension into 3 or 4 flasks. Alternatively, you make take the remaining cell suspension for an experiment.
